meta.title: THE EIGHTH PASSENGER — LIFELINE CUT V6 FINAL

event4.text: The one the archive said would remember.

event6.text: It's outside the broken relay spine, caught in the antenna frame like something tried to leave and got grabbed on the way out.

event8.text: The beacon is blinking in a rhythm. Not random. Not dying.

event11.text: Someone is tapping from the other side of a wall, and I hate that I know what hope sounds like now.

event14.text: There. Tell me you heard that too.

choice1.text: GUIDE MAYA CLOSER TO THE POD.

event3.text: I know I sound like I want to sprint at it, but that's mostly because my brain is terrible at grief.

event5.text: I'm setting the relay pack against a support strut and routing the scan through the pod beacon.

event7.text: I need to keep the antenna aligned. If I talk, I lose the return.

event3.text: I had to hold the relay steady against the strut the whole time. My shoulder is numb, but the data is clean.

event4.title: POD MANIFEST
event4.content:
Occupant: UNKNOWN
Life Support: 23%
Pressure: unstable but contained
Beacon Origin: external emergency pod
Visual Feed: unavailable
Manual Access: possible
Warning: identity not confirmed
Carrier Residue: 7.14 MHz harmonic
event4.caption: Remote scan confirms an active life-support pod with an unknown occupant.

event6.text: Unknown occupant. Critical status. Manual access possible.

event8.text: That last part feels like the station putting a knife in my hand and calling it a tool.

event3.text: I'm clipping myself to the service rail. If I drift away, pretend this was heroic and not deeply stupid.

event5.text: The gantry is exposed. Broken antenna ribs on the left. Open dark on the right.

event7.text: I need both hands for the crossing.

event2.text: I'm close enough to see frost on the hatch.

event4.text: No visual through the port. Just my reflection looking like someone who has made several bad decisions in a row.

event6.text: The pod corrected its own drift while I was crossing.

event8.text: Not much. Just enough to prove it isn't passive wreckage.

event1.text: There are two ways to reach the hatch.

event3.text: Short path across the exposed antenna rib. Fast, ugly, probably haunted by physics.

event5.text: Long path through the maintenance tether. Slower, less exposed, but the tether housing is damaged.

event7.text: I would love a third option called wake up somewhere safe.

event3.text: I'm stepping onto the rib. It is narrower than it looked from back there.

event5.text: No talking while I cross. If I hear myself panic, I may start agreeing with it.

event4.text: The rib flexed under me. I said a word I will not repeat because even the vacuum deserves dignity.

event6.text: My tether line snapped tight once. If the rail had failed, we would be having a very different conversation.

event8.text: But I'm here. Faster than the careful version of me would approve.

event3.text: Sensible. I hate how much I appreciate sensible right now.

event5.text: I'm checking handholds and suit line integrity as I go.

event7.text: This will take longer. Don't make a speech while I'm gone. I won't hear it.

event3.text: I found scratches inside the tether housing. Fresh enough that the dust hasn't settled in them.

event5.text: Something else used this route. Or someone dragged something through it.

event7.text: Good news: my line is intact. Bad news: my imagination is not.

event3.text: The pod is vibrating. Tiny pulses through the frame.

event5.text: Not enough to move it. Enough to feel like it has a heartbeat.

event8.text: Hale just went very quiet. That's never good.

event14.text: There it is. The calm voice.

event16.text: Maya, please. That pod should not be here.

event3.text: I know how I sound. I know I sound like a man making fear wear a lab coat.

event5.text: But if the pod is correcting its own drift without visible guidance, it is not passive wreckage.

event11.text: I'm running the hatch integrity check from here.

event2.title: POD HATCH INTEGRITY
event2.content:
Seal: intact
External frost: fresh
Manual lever: responsive
Motion correction: active
Carrier residue: 7.14 MHz harmonic
Risk: opening may trigger automatic occupant revival sequence
event2.caption: Hatch check shows active 7.14 residue and a possible automatic revival sequence.
event2.message: [POD HATCH INTEGRITY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event6.text: That's a phrase that should require a permit.

event1.text: I'm putting my hand on the hatch.

event3.text: It's colder than it should be. Even through the glove.

event6.text: No. No, don't say my name.

event8.text: The hatch display woke up when I touched it.

event9.title: POD CONTACT WAKE
event9.content:
Manual contact detected
Display state: partial wake
Suit registry ping returned
Recovered ID fragment: NV-08
Warning: occupant identity unresolved
event9.caption: Manual contact wakes the pod display and returns suit fragment NV-08.
event9.message: [POD CONTACT WAKE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event13.text: Tell me this isn't what I think it is.

event3.text: The manifest says unknown occupant.

event5.text: The suit fragment says NV-08.

event7.text: The life signs say someone is alive.

event10.text: I don't need a matching module to understand why that scares me.

event12.text: Noah Voss used NV-08 on everything.

event14.text: Tool tags. Cable caps. Ration boxes.

event16.text: He said being prepared was just anxiety with stationery.

event2.title: DEATH RECORD: NOAH VOSS
event2.content:
Subject: Noah Voss
Status: deceased
Reported Date: before Incident Alpha
Body Recovery: confirmed by Engineering Lead
Witness: Ethan Hale
Conflict: active biometric signal currently transmitting under suit ID NV-08
event2.caption: Recovered death record directly conflicts with pod telemetry.
event2.message: [DEATH RECORD: NOAH VOSS ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event8.text: Frost on the visor. Suit seal locked. His hand still curled like he was about to argue with someone.

event16.text: Most days I needed the second one more.

event1.text: I'm putting the mic against the hull.

event3.text: No one breathe dramatically. That includes me.

event5.text: I need silence for this. Real silence.

event8.text: You keep saying that like stepping back has ever saved anyone here.

event3.text: Twenty-three became twenty-one while we were arguing.

event5.text: If we delay too long, whoever is inside dies for real. Or dies again. I hate this sentence.

event7.text: If we open it wrong, the pod could depressurize or wake up whatever signal is riding it.

event9.text: I don't want plans. I want a decision.

event11.text: Fast rescue means I open the hatch and pull the occupant line. Controlled breach means we clamp first and risk the clock.

choice0.text: FAST RESCUE: OPEN THE HATCH NOW.

event3.text: Good. Terrible. Good.

event5.text: I'm releasing the manual latch and pulling the occupant line.

event4.text: Pressure kicked back. My shoulder hit the frame.

event8.text: That was too quick. She could have lost the seal.

event3.text: Thank you for choosing the option least likely to turn Maya into a cautionary diagram.

event5.text: I heard that.

event7.text: I'm anchoring the hatch while he calculates pressure release.

event3.text: My hands are shaking. Not from fear. From cold. Mostly.

event5.text: You can admit fear.

event11.text: Helmet is cracked. Not enough to kill him if the pod kept pressure, but enough to make the universe feel personal.

event14.text: The faceplate is fogged from the inside.

event18.text: Inside means I have to look.

event10.text: Maya, step away from the pod.

event12.text: Don't you dare make this smaller than it is.

event14.text: His eyes are moving under the lids.

event3.text: Thank you for making me do the thing that doesn't involve staring at a possibly dead friend's face.

event4.title: POD BIOMETRIC WRIST TRACE
event4.content:
Suit ID: NV-08
Heartbeat: active irregular
Neural response: suppressed
Body temperature: survivable
Identity confidence: 61% Noah Voss
Warning: memory imprint contamination detected
event4.caption: Wrist biometrics suggest Noah Voss but warn of memory imprint contamination.
event4.message: [POD BIOMETRIC WRIST TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event8.text: That's not enough to celebrate. It is absolutely enough to ruin me.

event12.text: There it is. The phrase that makes everything worse.

event3.text: If you disconnect him from the pod, whatever is sustaining the signal may come with him.

event5.text: If you leave him connected, the pod may stabilize him but keep broadcasting to 7.14.

event9.text: Save the body and risk bringing the signal in. Seal the pod and keep him alive but trapped.

event14.text: I need you to make the call before I make the emotional one.

choice1.text: SEAL POD AND KEEP HIM ALIVE.

event3.text: Noah, if that's you, I'm going to be furious when you wake up.

event5.text: I'm disconnecting the pod umbilicals and pulling him from the cradle.

event9.text: Not hard. Like he was checking if I was real.

event12.text: The pod just went dark behind us.

event4.text: I hate you a little for making the smart call.

event6.text: Not forever. Maybe not even for an hour.

event10.text: I'm rerouting reserve oxygen and locking the 7.14 bleed channel.

event3.text: He is alive. He is trapped. Those are both true.

event5.text: The pod still taps against the frame when the pressure cycles.

event7.text: Sometimes survival is just a kinder word for delay.

event9.text: Don't philosophize at me right now.

event1.text: I'm hauling him back through the tether.

event3.text: He is heavier than he looks. Or I am more tired than I want to admit.

event6.text: I need both hands. I'm moving now.

event3.text: His hand is still around my sleeve.

event5.text: Noah?

event10.text: He said my name like he was afraid it belonged to someone else.

choice1.text: LET NOAH REST AND MONITOR HIM.

event1.text: I'm backing away from the pod.

event3.text: I keep expecting him to knock from inside.

event10.text: Noah used to do that on bulkheads when he wanted attention.

event12.text: Or something in there knows that.

event14.text: I need to get out of the pod frame before I change my mind.

event1.text: I'm clear of the pod frame.

event3.text: The beacon is steadier now. That should make me feel better.

event5.text: It doesn't.

event7.text: I can still hear the tapping in the tether line. Maybe it is vibration. Maybe I am lying to myself.

event3.text: Noah. Stay with me. Look at my hand if you can't look at my face.

event5.text: He's trying to speak.

event11.text: Hale just stopped breathing on the channel.

event3.text: I hate that the right thing can look so much like abandoning someone.

event5.text: His pulse is ugly but present.

event7.text: He said one thing before his eyes closed again.

event11.text: Hale, why does that name make the signal colder?

event3.text: I keep repeating that because maybe if I say it enough, it becomes a strategy instead of a wound.

event5.text: The pod is alive. Noah is alive. The channel is not quiet.

event12.text: We need to find Eva Thorne.

event6.text: And I think the station just told us who it erased before him.

choice0.text: MOVE NOAH TO A SAFE ZONE.

event4.text: Operator, Noah is still in the pod.

event6.text: And whatever is speaking through it just gave us a name.

choice0.text: LEAVE THE POD AND FIND EVA.
